figures.caption: Figure 2: Abbreviated system prompts for the full and minimal strategies. Both specify the interface, hardware context, and scoring function. The full prompt (a) names several replacement techniques from prior work; the minimal prompt (b) poses open questions without prescribing solutions.
Figure 4: Per-trace IPC speedup over LRU for cache replacement policies, including state-of-the-art policies and the evolved policy.
Figure 5: Per-trace IPC speedup over no prefetching for data prefetching policies, including state-of-the-art policies and the evolved policy.
Figure 3: Geomean IPC speedup across all three domains. Left: cache replacement vs. LRU. Center: prefetch vs. no prefetching. Right: branch prediction vs. Bimodal.
Figure 13: Storage–performance Pareto frontier across evaluated policies. X-axis: storage cost, Y-axis: geomean IPC speedup.
Figure 8: Framework comparison across all three domains. Each panel shows geomean IPC speedup for the seed policy, the best OpenEvolve result, and the best AdaEvolve result.

Figure 1: Overview of the Agentic Architect framework and the evolution loop. Human icons denote inputs provided by the human architect: the system prompt, seed policy, evaluator function score, and trace database with its training/evaluation split.

Figure 9: Minimal vs. full prompt comparison for branch prediction evolution using AdaEvolve (Hashed Perceptron seed, 100 iterations). The y-axis shows the best combined evaluator score found so far at each iteration.
